name: @gotong/example-graded-trust
description: GT capstone (分级信任 track) — 一条边的信任是长出来的,审批摩擦随它变. The whole graded-trust chain in one deterministic run, no network, no key, no LLM: a freshly-handshaken peer lands on the fail-closed floor T1 (every action needs a human) → owner PINs its signing key, a re-verify returns pin_verified and suggestTierFromIdentity proposes T2 — but the suggestion never auto-applies (the effective tier stays T1 until the owner accepts) → owner elevates to T3 and the SAME action's approval keeps getting lighter, yet the most dangerous action still needs a member confirmation even at T3 (trust lowers friction, never removes the floor) → the now-T3 partner refers a brand-new hub-Z and suggestTierFromReferral proposes the FLOOR T1, not T3 (trust is not transitive). Real @gotong/core decideTrust / suggestTierFrom* pure functions underneath; nothing stubbed.
